active_question: Can a white-box influence or curvature-proxy observable provide a distinct membership signal beyond existing GSA-derived surfaces?

known_bad_default.reason: not the runnable GSA workspace for this asset identity

candidate_observable.name: diagonal-Fisher self-influence / curvature proxy
candidate_observable.required_raw_signal: per-sample gradient vector or per-channel gradient vector at fixed timestep and fixed noise
candidate_observable.minimum_implementation_scope: selected-layer raw parameter gradients; full-model Fisher/HVP/K-FAC is out of scope for the first scout
candidate_observable.shadow_estimator: diagonal Fisher or curvature proxy estimated only from shadow-side gradients
candidate_observable.score_family: g^T (F + lambda)^-1 g or an explicitly equivalent diagonal preconditioned gradient energy
allowed_inputs: member/nonmember split labels only for shadow fitting and held-out evaluation

must_not_be: scalar diffusion reconstruction loss under a new name
plain gradient norm under a new name
prior activation-subspace or channel-mask observable under a new name
required_baselines: activation-subspace or masked-observability packet when available
distinctness_gate.release_rule: GPU stays blocked unless a CPU micro-board shows a distinct extractor, comparable baselines, and a positive held-out shadow/target signal.

required_outputs: feature dimensionality after truncation or projection
cpu_micro_board_contract.raw_artifact_policy: do not persist raw gradient tensors by default; records may contain scalar scores and feature dimensionality only

next_steps: Implement a CPU micro-board extractor only if it can retain non-scalar gradient coordinates.
Close as blocked if the available code path only emits scalar loss or gradient norms.
Release no GPU task until the CPU micro-board artifact exists and passes validator review.
